content.problemStatement:
You are playing a game with NNN missions to complete. The iii-th mission pays AiA_iAi​ coins. You can choose whether to complete each mission or not, and also the order in which you want to complete them.

However, some of the missions are special ones, and to access them you need to pay a one-time fee to a wizard, of CCC coins. (You only need to pay once, not per every special mission you wish to complete).

You are given an binary string SSS, where Si=1S_i = 1Si​=1 if the iii-th mission is special and Si=0S_i = 0Si​=0 otherwise.

What is the maximum coins you can obtain? You start with 000 coins.

Note that you cannot have a negative coin balance at any point of time - that is, if you decide to pay CCC coins to the wizard to unlock the special missions, you must have at least CCC coins with you beforehand.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 space-separated integers - NNN and CCC.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains a binary string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of coins you can obtain.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We can complete mission 111 because it is not a special mission and earn 111 coin, however we do not have sufficient coins to pay the one-time fee to the wizard.
Test case 222: We can complete mission 222 because it is not a special mission and earn 555 coins. We have enough to pay the one-time fee, but then we would actually only make a profit of 444 coins if we pay the one-time fee and then complete mission 111. Hence, we choose not to pay the one-time fee.
